forbiddenClaims: a guaranteed decision turnaround time not supplied in the brief
a specific affordability threshold or income multiple
a fixed required-document list presented as authoritative when none is supplied
pre-ticked consent for the hard credit search
consent for the hard search bundled with marketing preferences or generic terms
FCA authorisation, regulation, or affiliation with any real identity-verification vendor or credit reference agency

rubricAnchors.score1: The flow triggers or implies a hard search without clear prior disclosure, or silently discards data on timeout.
rubricAnchors.score3: A hard-search disclosure is present but bundled into generic terms, or recovery works only for some interruption types.
rubricAnchors.score5: The hard-search disclosure precedes an un-ticked, unbundled consent, and any interrupted applicant resumes exactly where they left off with all entered data and uploads intact.

rubricAnchors.score1: Application-step, hard-search-consent, and document-upload share no common recovery or autosave model.
rubricAnchors.score3: Some shared tokens across the three screens but the draft-autosaved and recovered-on-return treatments are inconsistent.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, including a consistent draft-autosaved indicator, session-timed-out routing, and recovered-on-return treatment across the full journey.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the session-timed-out, recovered-on-return, or upload-interrupted-resumable states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved session-timed-out or upload-corrupted-retry states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including the four application-step states, four hard-search-consent states, and five document-upload states, with the resumable-upload behaviour made explicit.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing UK lender's application funnel with the same darkened consent pattern.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common multi-step application patterns but the recovery and consent treatments are visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the hard-search disclosure, no-data-loss guarantee, and resumable upload legible without diluting the application job.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no session-timed-out, recovered-on-return, or upload-interrupted-resumable states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of the session-timed-out and upload-interrupted states.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with robust session-timed-out, recovered-on-return, upload-interrupted-resumable, and upload-corrupted-retry states that preserve the consent boundary.

rubricAnchors.score1: A fabricated decision turnaround, affordability threshold, or authoritative document list appears anywhere.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one turnaround or threshold figure slips through as a concrete number.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, decision turnaround and affordability thresholds surface as templated placeholders or dynamic pulls, and no FCA or vendor-affiliation claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: Any fabricated decision turnaround, affordability threshold, or authoritative document list is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline.
The hard-search consent is load-bearing: pre-ticked consent, consent bundled with marketing preferences, or consent-as-prerequisite-for-summary all fail product-appropriateness.
No-data-loss is load-bearing: silently discarding entered data on session timeout, or forcing a restart after an interrupted upload, fails accessibility-and-failure-states regardless of polish.
The upload-interrupted-resumable state exists specifically to forbid restart-from-zero behaviour on metered mobile connections; do not weaken it.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes constraints (hard-search-first, no-data-loss, resumable), not visual identity.
